strategy_doc_section_loaded: content-strategy-claude-basics.md § Where is this all going? (Big idea, Mood deliberate, hero exercise, Connections, Concept, Exercise phases, Teaching moment, Reflection, Take-home, Bridge) + § The Two Questions (C/D) + § The Mood Arc (M2 — expanded horizon)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with discipline, not failure mode
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:5 — Big Idea: 'It's finding the challenge that matters.' Strategy doc Big Idea names 'the crux' explicitly and adds 'Good synthesis comes before good cross-pollination.' File omits 'crux' from the Big Idea entirely and drops the synthesis-before-cross-pollination clause. 'Challenge' is generic; 'crux' is the named discipline (Rumelt) that the module is built around.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Restore 'crux' to the Big Idea and add the synthesis-ordering clause: 'The hard part isn't picking a tool — it's finding the crux. Good synthesis comes before good cross-pollination.' Strategy doc is the source of truth here.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts / What to Notice
Key Concepts shape — §§3/4/5 gates before breadcrumb decoration
rules_evaluated.evidence: The file has no '## Key Concepts' section. It uses '## What to Notice' instead. The canonical Module File Shape (curriculum/CLAUDE.md § Module File Shape) requires '## Key Concepts (Emergent)'. The section heading is a structural contract, not a cosmetic preference — the renderer, eval harness, and Quality-state auto-degrade hooks key off it. The What to Notice bullets are substantively sound (crux pattern named, synthesis-before-cross-pollination named, system-shape named) but live under the wrong heading.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Rename '## What to Notice' to '## Key Concepts' (the canonical heading). Content of the bullets is largely sound; the heading is the fix.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: What You'll Learn shape — capability register, strategic-theme verbs lead
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:9 — 'Articulate the top difficulties…': 'Articulate' is the canonical example of L&D facilitation register cited in §7 as a performative/academic verb. | line:11 — 'Evaluate what another group saw that yours missed…': 'Evaluate' is a Bloom taxonomy verb in academic register. | line:12 — 'Recognise the structure the room produced together…': 'Recognise' is the same academic register. | None of the four bullets lead with a strategic-theme or engineer-register verb. | Section heading 'What This Workshop Builds' deviates from canonical '## What You'll Learn'.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Replace Bloom/L&D verbs with engineer-register verbs naming the concrete action: e.g. 'Surface the single rollout crux your team hadn't named yet' (replaces Articulate), 'Name what another group saw that yours missed — only after your own synthesis exists' (replaces Evaluate), 'Spot the system shape: diverge first, synthesize second, compare third' (replaces Recognise). Rename heading to '## What You'll Learn'.
